title: Build library/module for python
body:
Dear,

I had a similar Issue already open as I would like to use the verilator code within python.

I implemented two solutions for that.
a) Building a shared lib with the --create-lib function and then building a python wrapper that uses ctypes
b) Building a static lib with --create-lib function and then building cython code that can be compiled to a python modules with the static lib linked into it.

I think that Version b is smarter (but needs a cython compile setup) as I extended it with a function in order to simulate with input arrays from numpy (for speedup).
From my point of view - I could be easier to generate the .pxy and .pxd files by verilator directly as I take all required data from the V<module_name>.h file.
A small remark: Version b links against the static dpi_library but uses the classical C++ class instead of the DPI functions. That is a bit strange but saves me to understand the build process/the make files of verilator.

As you provide verilator - a great tool - I would like to provide the two generation scripts. Maybe someone else likes it. Sorry, I don't ave the knowledge to integrate it and to provide a complete pull request.
[Files.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/8311370/Files.zip)

Preparation: use verilator --create-lib  (this example used a module "cdc_rnm"
a)
Create the python module: dpi_interface_generator.py -py ../model/obj_dir/cdc_rnm.sv 
Example of the created code: cdc_rnm_dpi.py

b)
Create and build the module: python3 setup.py build_ext --inplace
Example of the created code: Pycdc_rnm.pxd, Pycdc_rnm.pxy (automatic generated)
